destinations.evidence: ExecutionStage stores an optional blocking handler and invokes it once per executed block with a Chain built from the executed block, receipts, and requests.
Pipeline construction wires the configured handler into the execution stage.
The engine launcher creates a pool side-effect handler and passes it into the backfill pipeline.

equivalence_argument: Backfill execution invokes the configured blocking handler for each executed block using a Chain built from execution output.
notes: The handler is optional and only active when the launcher wires it into the pipeline.
rationale: The diff adds with_blocking_handler to ExecutionStage, threads the handler through pipeline construction, creates a backfill handler in EngineNodeLauncher, and calls the handler after block execution. No candidate in this bounded assignment contradicts or removes it.
statement: When a blocking handler is configured on ExecutionStage, backfill execution must call it for each executed block using a Chain built from the block and execution result receipts/requests.
suggested_falsification_tests: Run an execution stage with a test blocking handler over two blocks and assert the handler is called once per block with the expected block number/hash and receipts/requests-derived Chain.
summary: Backfill execution invokes the blocking handler.
test_or_falsification_candidate: Run ExecutionStage with a test blocking handler over two blocks and assert two calls with matching block/receipts data.
title: Backfill execution invokes blocking handler
